title: Admins may see stale or delayed Microsoft 365 Copilot report metrics in the Microsoft 365 admin center
impact: Admins may have seen stale or delayed Microsoft 365 Copilot report metrics in the Microsoft 365 admin center.

updates.content:
Title: Admins may see stale or delayed Microsoft 365 Copilot report metrics in the Microsoft 365 admin center

User impact: Admins may see stale or delayed Microsoft 365 Copilot report metrics in the Microsoft 365 admin center.

More info: Copilot report metrics weren't appropriately refreshed with data after March 31, 2026. As a result, users may have seen stale or delayed metrics for data after this date until the data pipeline processing resumed normal operating speed.

Current status: Our service health monitoring alerted us to failures occurring within the portion of service infrastructure responsible for relaying Copilot reporting data to the Microsoft 365 admin center. We're investigating the affected infrastructure to identify the source of the failures.

Scope of impact: Any admin viewing Copilot usage reports in the Microsoft 365 admin center may be impacted.

Start time: Saturday, April 4, 2026, at 11:00 PM UTC

Root cause: A missing dependency service responsible for facilitating timely data delivery for the Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365) usage reports in the Microsoft 365 admin center is resulting in impact.

Next update by: Wednesday, April 8, 2026, at 12:00 AM UTC

Title: Admins may see stale or delayed Microsoft 365 Copilot report metrics in the Microsoft 365 admin center

User impact: Admins may see stale or delayed Microsoft 365 Copilot report metrics in the Microsoft 365 admin center.

More info: Copilot report metrics weren't appropriately refreshed with data after March 31, 2026. As a result, users may have seen stale or delayed metrics for data after this date until the data pipeline processing resumed normal operating speed.

Current status: We've reinstated the missing dependency and are processing the backlog of Copilot report metrics. We expect that the missing data will be available and the impact will be remediated by the time of our next update.

Scope of impact: Any admin viewing Copilot usage reports in the Microsoft 365 admin center may be impacted.

Start time: Saturday, April 4, 2026, at 11:00 PM UTC

Root cause: A missing dependency service responsible for facilitating timely data delivery for the Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365) usage reports in the Microsoft 365 admin center is resulting in impact.

Next update by: Thursday, April 9, 2026, at 12:00 AM UTC

Title: Admins may see stale or delayed Microsoft 365 Copilot report metrics in the Microsoft 365 admin center

User impact: Admins may have seen stale or delayed Microsoft 365 Copilot report metrics in the Microsoft 365 admin center.

More info: Copilot report metrics weren't appropriately refreshed with data after March 31, 2026. As a result, users may have seen stale or delayed metrics for data after this date until the data pipeline processing resumed normal operating speed.
 
Final status: We've processed the backlog of Copilot report metrics and confirmed using our internal telemetry data that impact is remediated.
 
Scope of impact: Any admin viewing Copilot usage reports in the Microsoft 365 admin center may have been impacted.
 
Start time: Saturday, April 4, 2026, at 11:00 PM UTC
 
End time: Wednesday, April 8, 2026, at 8:38 PM UTC
 
Root cause: A missing dependency service responsible for facilitating timely data delivery for the Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365) usage reports in the Microsoft 365 admin center resulted in impact.
 
Next steps: 
- We're reviewing and improving the service responsible for facilitating timely data delivery for the Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365) usage reports to prevent similar incidents from occurring again in the future.
 
This is the final update for the event.
